completed_tasks.name: Debug investigation — angle frame audit
completed_tasks.notes: gsd-debugger produced full Root Cause Report: 7 bugs found, all trace to migration script not rotating Fisher covariance

completed_tasks.name: Physics-change protocol — covariance rotation formula
completed_tasks.notes: 5 protocol items presented and APPROVED by user. Formula: Sigma_ecl = J_rot * Sigma_eq * J_rot^T, cross-terms v_ecl = J_rot * v_eq. Reference: Taylor An Introduction to Error Analysis Eq. 3.47

remaining_tasks.notes: PHYSICS CHANGE — approved. Add _compute_ecliptic_jacobian + _rotate_covariance_block + update migrate_csv for 3 states (fully migrated / pos-only migrated / unmigrated). Also add _cov_frame column and update idempotency guard.

remaining_tasks.name: Run full test suite + commit
remaining_tasks.notes: uv run pytest -m 'not gpu and not slow'. Then [PHYSICS] commit.

decisions.decision: Covariance rotation formula: Sigma_ecl = J_rot * Sigma_eq * J_rot^T, J_rot computed via central finite differences (delta=1e-7 rad) using astropy ICRS->BarycentricTrueEcliptic transform
decisions.rationale: Standard error propagation under coordinate transform. J_rot per source because the rotation is position-dependent. Central differences more accurate than forward-diff.

decisions.decision: Migration script handles 3 states: (1) fully migrated (both _coord_frame + _cov_frame) -> skip, (2) pos-only migrated (_coord_frame but no _cov_frame) -> load .bak_equatorial backup for original equatorial positions to compute J_rot, then rotate covariance only, (3) unmigrated -> full migration
decisions.rationale: Production CSVs in simulations/ were migrated by old script (Phase 43) — they have _coord_frame but no _cov_frame. Backup .bak_equatorial always exists for these.

decisions.decision: Detection.__init__ frame guard is a hard ValueError (not just warning)
decisions.rationale: Silent frame mismatch is the whole bug. Hard error prevents future silent data corruption. Tests updated to include frame columns.

decisions.decision: Bug scope: bugs 1-5 apply ONLY to pre-Phase-43 CSVs that were migrated with old script. New simulation runs (post-Phase-43) are already correct — Fisher is computed with ecliptic qS/phiS so covariance is natively ecliptic.
decisions.rationale: Key insight from debugger: Fisher matrix is computed by numerically differentiating waveform at current qS/phiS values. After Phase 43 those are already ecliptic.

next_action: Implement scripts/migrate_crb_to_ecliptic.py: add _compute_ecliptic_jacobian() and _rotate_covariance_block() helpers, update migrate_csv() to handle 3 states, add _COV_FRAME constant and _cov_frame column. Physics change approved.
context_notes: Physics-change protocol completed and approved. All 5 items presented: old formula (migrate_crb_to_ecliptic.py:98-100 only rotates qS/phiS), new formula (Sigma_ecl=J_rot*Sigma_eq*J_rot^T + cross-term rotation), reference (Taylor Eq.3.47), dimensional analysis (J_rot dimensionless, Sigma in rad^2), limiting case (zero obliquity -> identity). Bug scope: 7 bugs found by debugger, all downstream of missing covariance rotation in migration script. Bugs 1-5 affect migrated pre-Phase-43 data; new simulations are already correct. MAP=0.730 is valid.
